Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. TruffleRuby 96 upvotes, 4 comments
  2. From 300KB to 69KB per Token: How LLM Architectures Solve the KV Cache Problem 107 upvotes, 9 comments
  3. TinyLoRA – Learning to Reason in 13 Parameters 142 upvotes, 17 comments
  4. Learn Something Old Every Day, Part XVIII: How Does FPU Detection Work? 40 upvotes, 4 comments
  5. MiniStack (replacement for LocalStack) 189 upvotes, 37 comments
  6. 4D Doom 173 upvotes, 39 comments
  7. Back to FreeBSD – Part 2 – Jails 69 upvotes, 13 comments
  8. Teenage Engineering's PO-32 acoustic modem and synth implementation 101 upvotes, 22 comments
  9. We intercepted the White House app's network traffic 195 upvotes, 58 comments
  10. A dot a day keeps the clutter away 248 upvotes, 77 comments
  11. Open source CAD in the browser (Solvespace) 318 upvotes, 103 comments
  12. Digitizing photos from the 1998 Game Boy Camera 19 upvotes, 2 comments
  13. Cohere Transcribe: Speech Recognition 180 upvotes, 55 comments
  14. Show HN: Postgres extension for BM25 relevance-ranked full-text search 128 upvotes, 38 comments
  15. Bring Back MiniDV with This Raspberry Pi FireWire Hat 29 upvotes, 5 comments
  16. The Claude Code Source Leak: fake tools, frustration regexes, undercover mode 1077 upvotes, 417 comments
  17. Claude Code Unpacked : A visual guide 86 upvotes, 24 comments
  18. Axios compromised on NPM – Malicious versions drop remote access trojan 1811 upvotes, 735 comments
  19. Ordinary Lab Gloves May Have Skewed Microplastic Data 87 upvotes, 28 comments
  20. Show HN: 1-Bit Bonsai, the First Commercially Viable 1-Bit LLMs 195 upvotes, 81 comments
  21. Mad Bugs: Vim vs. Emacs vs. Claude 7 upvotes, 0 comments
  22. Neanderthals survived on a knife's edge for 350k years 97 upvotes, 42 comments
  23. I traced my traffic through a home Tailscale exit node 104 upvotes, 48 comments
  24. Chess in SQL 17 upvotes, 4 comments
  25. Butterfly-collecting: The history of an insult (2017) 4 upvotes, 0 comments
  26. OpenAI closes funding round at an $852B valuation 405 upvotes, 353 comments
  27. Analyzing Geekbench 6 under Intel's BOT 16 upvotes, 11 comments
  28. Use string views instead of passing std:wstring by const& 34 upvotes, 33 comments
  29. Slop is not necessarily the future 213 upvotes, 365 comments
  30. Why the US Navy won't blast the Iranians and 'open' Strait of Hormuz 252 upvotes, 668 comments

Last generated 2:30pm, 1 April 2026. Source here.
